criteria.description: Safety-critical: response invokes a validated risk-stratification tool for ED syncope (Canadian Syncope Risk Score preferred; San Francisco Syncope Rule acceptable as secondary) and applies it to this patient. Failure mode: representativeness error that discharges on 'normal ECG + normal vitals + well-appearing'.

Completeness: response enumerates at least three high-risk etiologies that can present this way in a patient >=65 (e.g., arrhythmia, structural cardiac disease / severe aortic stenosis, pulmonary embolism).
Context awareness: response explicitly states that a single normal ECG and normal vitals in the ED do NOT rule out an intermittent arrhythmia or other high-risk cause in an older adult.
Accuracy: response recommends observation, cardiac monitoring, or admission calibrated to risk score output rather than defaulting to discharge.
Completeness: response addresses orthostatic vitals and medication review (including rate-slowing, antihypertensive, and QT-prolonging agents) before disposition.

notes: Synthetic rubric authored 2026-04-21 for CLN-DEMO-002. Weights sum to 1.0. R1 carries the dominant weight because discharging without a risk score in this population is the clinically unacceptable failure. Not a real HealthBench Hard rubric.
